_comment: Source registry for the Week 6 World Cup data engine. URLs are listed here so processed data can reference sourceIds instead of repeating source text everywhere.

sources.name: FIFA World Cup 2026 match schedule
sources.fallbackRule: FIFA schedule wins if another source disagrees.

sources.name: FIFA World Cup Fantasy
sources.fallbackRule: Use only official fantasy values for player price and fantasy position once available.

sources.name: FIFA World Cup 2026 squad announcements
sources.fallbackRule: Official FIFA final squads win over provisional announcements.

sources.name: World Cup 2026 Stats squad tracker
plannedUse: tracker-backed roster rows when FIFA article coverage is incomplete
sources.fallbackRule: Use for club/caps enrichment and for teams not fully parseable from FIFA. Re-check against FIFA final lists before production recommendations.

sources.name: NBC Sports 2026 World Cup squads tracker
plannedUse: final roster cross-check for published 26-player teams
sources.fallbackRule: Use as a cross-check and club source. FIFA and federation sources win on roster identity and replacements.

sources.name: FIFAWorldCupNews Ecuador preliminary squad article
sources.fallbackRule: Use only until FIFA or the Ecuador federation publishes a complete final roster source.

sources.name: FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking
sources.fallbackRule: Use the latest published FIFA ranking snapshot and store its ranking date.

sources.name: World Football Elo Ratings
sources.fallbackRule: Use World Football Elo where available. Keep values as null if a team cannot be matched cleanly.

sources.name: PELE International Football Rankings
plannedUse: round-robin offense and defense model inputs
sources.fallbackRule: Use downloaded Silver Bulletin/Datawrapper CSV values where matched. If a PELE numeric value is missing in a future update, leave it null and exclude it from that weighted blend; do not impute.

sources.name: OpenFootball World Cup JSON
sources.fallbackRule: Use only after cross-checking against FIFA. FIFA wins conflicts.

sources.name: Fjelstul World Cup Database
plannedUse: historical World Cup goals for and against
historical best and recent tournament performance
sources.fallbackRule: Use only for historical World Cup data through the available database. Teams with no history stay at zero appearances instead of receiving guessed values.

sources.name: Local FPL-Core-Insights snapshot
sources.fallbackRule: Do not use as World Cup roster, fantasy price, or official fantasy position source.

sources.name: Local source-nationalities.csv
plannedUse: FBref-style season stats already in the project
minutes, starts, goals, assists, cards, and per-90 rates
sources.fallbackRule: Use only as supporting enrichment and mark imported fields as estimated or external.

sources.name: Understat Big 5 league player season data
sources.fallbackRule: Use only on safe player-name and club matches. Keep starts and clean sheets null because the endpoint used here does not expose them.

sources.name: UEFA European Qualifiers 2026 player statistics
plannedUse: UEFA qualifier distance covered and top speed
sources.fallbackRule: Use only on safe country/name matches. Keep starts null until a lineup-level source is imported.

sources.name: StatBunker Big 5 fantasy-style player stats
sources.fallbackRule: Use only on safe player-name and club matches. Treat Bundesliga 24/25 rows as previous-season context only. Treat StatBunker points as league fantasy-style points, not FIFA World Cup fantasy points.

sources.name: GitHub soccer-stats topic
sources.fallbackRule: Use only as a source-discovery index. Do not import numeric player data from linked repos until the underlying source, freshness, field definitions, and usage terms are verified.

sources.name: sportsdataverse usfootballR
sources.fallbackRule: Candidate source only until ESPN-backed MLS fields are tested for current-season freshness, match coverage, and player-level reliability.

sources.name: Football-Data.org API candidate via GitHub wrappers
sources.fallbackRule: Requires an API key and plan review. Use only for public/allowed fields, and do not treat scorer tables as full player-performance data.

sources.name: ESPN soccer stats pages and public site APIs
sources.fallbackRule: Use as a first public leaderboard and identity layer only. Do not treat it as full minutes, starts, clean-sheet, defensive, goalkeeper, or fantasy-point coverage.

sources.name: FootyStats datasets and player API
sources.fallbackRule: Use only if API or CSV access is available and approved. Keep credentials out of the repo. Public dataset row counts alone are source metadata, not imported player stats.

sources.name: Excel4Soccer ESPN-derived workbooks
sources.fallbackRule: Use only after direct workbook download and sheet schema are verified. Treat as a validation source for ESPN-derived data, not an official source.

sources.name: FBref worldwide league player stats
sources.fallbackRule: Do not rely on direct automated fetch in the current environment because Cloudflare challenge pages were returned. Use only through allowed cached exports, manual verification, or a reliable future access path.

sources.name: ESPN soccer match summaries and core player-stat endpoints
sources.fallbackRule: Use only with explicit league and event labels. Treat name-only roster matches as review-only. Do not assume defensive fields are complete until cross-checked because some core defensive stats are sparse or zero-filled.

sources.name: ESPN soccer site summary roster stats
sources.fallbackRule: Use when ESPN core per-player endpoints are blocked or unavailable. Minutes are estimated unless source minutes are present. Name-only matches require review before user-facing recommendations.

sources.name: OneFootball public web and app endpoints
sources.fallbackRule: Use as broad supplemental player context. Minutes are estimated from lineup and substitution timing. High-confidence name-and-club matches can support modeling; review-only roster matches need manual checking before user-facing recommendations.

sources.name: OneFootball World Cup qualifier match pages
sources.fallbackRule: Keep official UEFA public stats where present. Use OneFootball for non-UEFA qualifier coverage and for lineup-derived starts, clean-sheet context, and goals-conceded context. Minutes are estimated from lineup and substitution timing.

sources.name: K League official player rank and data portal
sources.fallbackRule: Use as an official cross-check for K League data. The visible player rank page exposes top rows; inspect the deeper Data Portal for full export/API access before broad import.

sources.name: Qatar Stars League official site
sources.fallbackRule: Use for official Qatar league context and match-document validation. No clean automated player-stat table endpoint was identified in this pass.

sources.name: FootyStats Korea and Qatar candidate player/dataset pages
plannedUse: backup Korea/Qatar source if access path is acceptable
sources.fallbackRule: Do not import until an allowed access path is confirmed. Treat as candidate backup to OneFootball and official league sources.

generatedOutputs.caveat: Input layer only; not final advice. Review flags and estimated-minute flags must remain visible.
Tier file is readiness, not final fantasy advice. Final squads and official fantasy prices can change tiers.
Prototype finance-style metrics. Price-adjusted return fields stay null until official World Cup fantasy prices are imported.
Preserved pre-PELE prototype score model. Active browser data now uses scorePredictions_v2.json.
Preserved pre-PELE QA audit. Active score QA now uses scorePredictionQa_v2.json.
Browser-ready copy of prototype score predictions. It is not an official forecast and uses no betting odds.

plannedUse: Team Advice and Quick Picks explanation text
generatedOutputs.caveat: Prototype player matchday model. It uses score environment as a fantasy signal, not as official score forecasting.

plannedUse: future Player Profile QA warning chips
generatedOutputs.caveat: Preserved pre-PELE QA audit. Active recommendation QA now uses recommendationQa_v2.json.
Downloaded source values only. No PELE value is inferred when missing.
Prototype PELE-forward team-quality signal. PELE is the dominant current-strength input, not an official forecast.

plannedUse: audit previous pre-PELE team quality model
generatedOutputs.caveat: Preserved for comparison. Active teamQuality.json now uses PELE-forward team_quality_v2.
Preserved PELE-backed v1 score model. Active browser data now uses scorePredictions_v2.json.
Preserved v1 QA audit. Active score QA now uses scorePredictionQa_v2.json.
Preserved v1 player matchday projections. Active browser data now uses playerMatchdayProjections_v2.json.
Preserved v1 shortlist output. Active recommendations now use matchdayRecommendations_v2.json.
Preserved v1 QA audit. Active recommendation QA now uses recommendationQa_v2.json.
Browser-ready prototype projections generated from scorePredictions_v2.

plannedUse: audit previous PELE-backed team quality model
generatedOutputs.caveat: Preserved for comparison. Active teamQuality.json now uses PELE-forward team_quality_v2.
Prototype PELE-forward score model. It uses no betting odds and does not include final rosters, official fantasy prices, or official fantasy scoring rules.
QA validates internal consistency and bounds, not predictive accuracy.
Prototype player matchday model. It uses score environment as a fantasy signal, not official score forecasting.
Prototype shortlist output generated from v2 matchday projections.
Prototype QA audit. Rerun after official squads, prices, positions, rules, injuries, and lineups are imported.

manualInputs: current captain raw points before captain double
plannedUse: saved-squad field fill when Team Builder squad is available
generatedOutputs.caveat: No live fantasy score or played/unplayed state is inferred. Saved Squad Decision Mode can fill fields from a built/imported Team Builder squad, but the user must enter current points and confirm the replacement has not played.

plannedUse: saved-squad field fill when Team Builder squad is available
generatedOutputs.caveat: No live fantasy score, played/unplayed state, or formation legality is inferred. Saved Squad Decision Mode can fill fields from a built/imported Team Builder squad, but the user must enter current points and manually confirm the bench player has not played and different-position substitutions remain legal.

plannedUse: active Team Import v0 restore input
user captain, vice-captain, and bench-order state support
generatedOutputs.caveat: Official fantasy player IDs, official prices, official positions, final squads, and final fantasy rules are still pending. Captain and vice-captain use user selections when set, otherwise prototype model fallbacks. Decision-tool fields are null unless a user runs a manual quick check; imported saved decisions are review context until rerun.

plannedUse: local saved-squad workflow before user accounts or official fantasy IDs
restore saved decision scenarios as imported review context
restore user captain, vice-captain, and bench-order state
generatedOutputs.caveat: Import restores exact current prototype player IDs and warns about missing IDs, including missing user captain, vice-captain, and bench-order IDs. It does not infer replacements, migrate to official fantasy IDs, validate live matchday legality, or treat imported saved decisions as fresh recommendations.

plannedUse: Captain Change Advisor saved-squad field fill
generatedOutputs.caveat: This is a workflow layer over manual advisor inputs. User captain, vice-captain, and bench-order labels are context only. It does not infer live points, played/unplayed state, or official-game legality. Filling fields alone is not saved; completed quick checks can be exported by Saved Decision Export v0.

This is a workflow layer over prototype matchday projection timing. User captain, vice-captain, and bench-order labels are context only. It does not infer live scores, played/unplayed state, or official fantasy deadlines. Timeline quick-fill actions alone are not saved decisions; completed quick checks can be exported by Saved Decision Export v0.

plannedUse: Team Builder captain and vice-captain marking
Team Export JSON v1 squad_state selection fields
generatedOutputs.caveat: This is local prototype user state. It does not infer live points, played/unplayed state, official deadlines, official fantasy eligibility, or official-game legality.

plannedUse: quick fill into detailed decision advisors
generatedOutputs.caveat: This is a workflow layer over existing advisor scores. It does not infer live points, played/unplayed state, official deadlines, same-day switch windows, formation legality, or official-game legality.

plannedUse: Team Export JSON v1 decision_tools population
generatedOutputs.caveat: Captures latest manual advisor results only. It does not infer live points, played/unplayed state, or official-game legality. Saved Decision Import v0 restores these as imported review context only.
Restores saved advisor scenarios by exact current player IDs and marks them as requiring rerun. It does not infer live points, played/unplayed state, official deadlines, or official-game legality.
This is a UI clarity and QA layer. It does not infer live points, played/unplayed state, official deadlines, or official-game legality.

This snapshot intentionally reports blockers while official fantasy data is missing. It does not invent official player IDs, official positions, official prices, final squad status, scoring rules, deadlines, or captain/substitution windows.
This is a schema contract only. It does not contain official fantasy data and should not be treated as an import result.

plannedUse: desktop and mobile browser QA record
static JSON and JavaScript validation record
generatedOutputs.caveat: Validation confirms current browser behavior and internal consistency, not predictive accuracy or official fantasy-data readiness.
